```{r}
# ============================================================
# Graph 5: Scatter Plot with Regression (Year vs Log Mass)
# "Quantifying the Trend"
# ============================================================

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(scales)

# Prepare data: filter for valid years and mass
trend_data <- meteorites_clean %>%
  filter(!is.na(year) & year >= 1800 & year <= 2013) %>%
  filter(!is.na(mass..g.) & mass..g. > 0) %>%
  # Filter to main types to keep the plot clean
  filter(meteorite_type %in% c("Stone", "Iron", "Stony-Iron"))

# Build the plot
ggplot(trend_data, aes(x = year, y = mass..g., color = meteorite_type)) +
  # Use high transparency (alpha) because of the sheer number of points
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2, size = 0.8) +
  # Add linear regression lines for each type
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, linewidth = 1.2) +
  # Log scale for Y axis to handle mass variance
  scale_y_log10(
    labels = function(x) {
      ifelse(x >= 1e6, paste0(x/1e6, " t"),
             ifelse(x >= 1000, paste0(x/1000, " kg"), paste0(x, " g")))
    },
    breaks = c(1, 100, 10000, 1000000)
  ) +
  # Use the palette from Graph 3 for consistency
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Stone" = "#2A9D8F", "Iron" = "#E63946", "Stony-Iron" = "#F4A261")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Quantifying the Downward Trend in Mass",
    subtitle = "Year of discovery vs. Mass (log scale). Lines represent linear regression by type.",
    x = "Year of Discovery",
    y = "Mass (log scale)",
    color = "Meteorite Type",
    caption = "Data Source: NASA Open Data Portal\nNote: The downward slope indicates increased sensitivity to smaller specimens over time."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "top"
  )


```
